channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Sindh Faces Water Shortage as Sharjeel Memon Urges Federal Action on Irsa's Distribution
item.description: Senior Minister Sharjeel Inam Memon has raised concerns over the Indus River System Authority's (Irsa) recent reduction of Sindh’s water allocation, calling it unjust and in violation of the 1991 Wate...

item.title: PSG retain Champions League title after penalty shootout win over Arsenal
item.description: Paris Saint-Germain successfully defended their UEFA Champions League crown after a tense and hard-fought final against Arsenal at the Puskas Arena. The match ended 1-1 after extra time, with PSG even...

item.title: GOP strategists warn Trump faces mounting political and economic challenges ahead of midterms
item.description: Republican strategists are raising alarms about President Donald Trump’s political standing as the 2026 midterm elections approach, citing both economic concerns and internal party tensions. During a ...

item.title: Nova Scotia craft brewers react cautiously to new direct-to-consumer alcohol trade deal with Ontario
item.description: Craft brewers in Nova Scotia are offering mixed reactions to a newly announced agreement between Nova Scotia and Ontario that allows alcohol producers to sell directly to consumers in each other’s pro...

item.title: Socceroos narrowly lose to Mexico in final pre-World Cup friendly
item.description: Australia's Socceroos faced a tough 1-0 loss against Mexico in their final warm-up match before the FIFA World Cup 2026. The match, held at the Rose Bowl stadium in Pasadena, California, saw Mexico do...

item.title: Government works on clarifying exemptions under proposed capital gains tax reforms
item.description: Housing Minister Clare O’Neil has committed to a swift resolution regarding which individuals and businesses will be eligible for exemptions from the proposed changes to the capital gains tax (CGT) di...

item.title: Mixx Launches Rewards Campaign for FIFA World Cup with Cash and Prizes for Tanzanian Users
item.description: The digital financial services platform Mixx has started giving prizes to its customers through a new campaign linked to the upcoming Fifa World Cup. This campaign, done together with Hisense company,...

item.title: African Traders Increasingly Invest in Gold and Silver Amid Economic Uncertainty
item.description: In 2026, trading behaviour across Africa is shifting noticeably as more traders dey focus on gold and silver. This change is largely due to global political tensions, market volatility, and currency i...

item.title: Nepal PM acknowledges mutual border encroachment concerns with India, calls for diplomatic resolution
item.description: Nepal Prime Minister Balendra Shah has stated in Parliament that both Nepal and India have encroached upon each other’s territories, bringing a nuanced perspective to the ongoing border dispute betwee...

item.title: Essendon navigates a transitional period under interim coach Dean Solomon
item.description: Essendon has entered a new phase under interim coach Dean Solomon following the sacking of Brad Scott with 18 months left on his contract. Solomon, a former club director and premiership teammate of J...

item.title: KWSC reports partial water supply disruption in Karachi due to K-Electric cable fault
item.description: Karachi Water and Sewerage Corporation (KWSC) has confirmed a significant water shortfall in the city following a technical fault in K-Electric's main cable supplying North East Karachi Pumping Statio...

item.title: New Brunswick Student Recreates Bill Reid’s Haida Art Using 15,000 Lego Bricks
item.description: Henry Campbell, a Grade 11 student at Sir James Dunn Academy in Saint Andrews, New Brunswick, has successfully recreated Bill Reid’s iconic Haida print 'Haida Dog Salmon – Skaagi' using 15,000 Lego pi...

item.title: PHLPost improves notification process to help customers track delayed parcels
item.description: The Philippine Postal Corporation (PHLPost) has recently announced measures to enhance communication with customers regarding international parcel deliveries. Delays often occur because packages are i...

item.title: NSCDC charges 24 suspects, including Chinese nationals, over alleged illegal lithium mining in Nasarawa
item.description: The Nigeria Security and Civil Defence Corps (NSCDC) Mining Marshals have brought 15 Chinese nationals and nine Nigerians before the Federal High Court in Abuja over alleged illegal mining activities ...

item.title: Critics from across the political spectrum challenge Maria Bartiromo’s claims about Kennedy Center naming dispute
item.description: Fox News host Maria Bartiromo faced criticism from journalists and commentators across the political spectrum after making inaccurate claims about a legal dispute involving the naming of the Kennedy C...

item.title: Governor Uba Sani leads APC’s Ekiti governorship campaign council
item.description: The All Progressives Congress (APC) don set up one strong National Campaign Council to prepare for the Ekiti State Governorship Election wey dey come. Kaduna State Governor, Uba Sani, na him dey lead ...

item.title: Why Contemporary Top 40 Music Often Sounds Similar
item.description: A close listen to today’s top 40 music reveals a striking similarity across tracks in terms of production, tempo, vocal style, and instrumentation. Compared to the more diverse charts of the 1980s, cu...

item.title: Power Minister confirms disciplinary action after corruption complaint in LESCO case
item.description: Power Minister Awais Leghari has confirmed that strict disciplinary action has been taken against a Lahore Electric Supply Company (LESCO) line superintendent following allegations of corruption highl...

item.title: Bafana legend urges support for Lyle Foster after missed penalty
item.description: Bafana Bafana legend Mark Williams has called on South African football fans to stop booing striker Lyle Foster. Foster, 25, recently missed a penalty in a warm-up match against Nicaragua, prompting s...

item.title: Israeli forces seize strategic site in Lebanon during rare cross-border incursion
item.description: According to reporting attributed to The Associated Press and published via CTV News, Israeli forces have carried out a cross-border operation into Lebanon in what is described as the deepest incursio...
